title: <div>  •  Test audio transitions for seamless looping to maintain immersion.</di
content:
• Test audio transitions for seamless looping to maintain immersion.
3. Educational Journey in 3D
Approach:
• Timewalk Onboarding: Create a cinematic fly-through using Unity’s Timeline for each era, narrated by the Archivist (TTS or pre-recorded voice). Keep it under 30 seconds to avoid overwhelming new players.
• In-World Tutorials: Use 3D UI canvases for scene-anchored prompts. Highlight valid moves with glowing checkers and animated trails (via Unity’s Particle System). Offer skippable tutorials for returning players.
• Challenge Scenarios: Implement puzzles as scripted scenes with historical avatars (e.g., Roman Centurion). Use Unity’s Animator for avatar gestures and dialogue triggers.
• Cultural Variants: Package rule variants (Nard, Tapa) as modular scripts (ruleset.js compiled to C#). Display micro-tutorials as 3D pop-ups with ghost move animations.
• Philosophical Prompts: Trigger brief quotes (e.g., Stoic maxims) as 3D text overlays after key moves, with a “Learn More” button linking to lore.
• Progress Tracking: Store skill maps and badges in Firebase Firestore, syncing progress across devices.
